decisions.decision: Gate by repo size (>500 Java files) + automation (delta), not by command
decisions.rationale: Data: gating core capability churns users; gating scale/collaboration ~doubles conversion. Free hooks solo devs; Pro charges enterprise monoliths.

decisions.decision: Java-only monetization (non-Java repos free at any size)
decisions.rationale: Product targets enterprise Java monoliths; is_large_repo counts only .java by design. Documented in license.py + PRODUCT_TIERS.md.

decisions.decision: Pro price 19 EUR/mo / 190 EUR/yr per-dev (down from 29.99)
decisions.rationale: CLI is focused tool, not platform; sit at CodeScene floor ($20-30) / Cursor norm ($20), not above. Adoption-oriented. Per-LOC/per-repo reserved for a future org tier.

decisions.decision: Only Free + Pro; no Enterprise tier
decisions.rationale: Nothing org-level built yet (SSO/dashboards/hosted MCP). Honest. Listed as roadmap in PRODUCT_TIERS.md.

decisions.decision: MCP local serve Free; hosted MCP = future Enterprise
decisions.rationale: Local serve already free in code; aligns README/landing.

next_action: Either update Lemon Squeezy pricing to 19/190, or push the three commits. Code work is complete and committed.
context_notes: Post-milestone ad-hoc work (all 13 GSD phases already 100%). Monetization model change driven by competitor research (CodeScene/SonarQube/Sourcegraph/Cursor) + SaaS gating best-practice data. Gate is client-side (cli.py) + Apache 2.0 — no DRM; moat = convenience, known/accepted for v1. atlas-cli on master, landing on master.
